source_jobs.title: Hospital Relationship Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ABBY CARE: POWERING THE FUTURE OF CARE AT HOME FOR ALL OF AMERICA.


Abby Care is building the leading AI-native platform for family-led care. America is facing a growing care crisis. Millions more people need care at home than ever. Over 50 million family caregivers support loved ones without the tools, training, or recognition they deserve.

We believe families are the largest untapped caregiving workforce in America, and that technology can help them deliver better care while driving stronger outcomes and greater transparency across the healthcare system.

Abby Care combines clinical oversight with an AI-powered platform to train, enable, and support family caregivers in delivering high-quality care at home. Our platform helps health plans and government partners better understand, verify, and improve care in the home. We expand access to care, reduce reliance on higher-cost settings, and help ensure public dollars are spent effectively.

We are proud to partner with leading health plans, providers, and community organizations and are backed by top VCs. We envision a future where family-led care is a core part of the healthcare system. Abby Care is building that future.

Join us in solving one of the most important challenges of our time.



The Role

At Abby Care, we are redefining how care is delivered at home, but that journey often starts in the hospital. The Clinical Liaison is the face of Abby Care within major hospitals. This is not a role for someone who wants to sit behind a desk or make corporate-level network calls; this is a highly active, embedded role. You will live within the four walls of your assigned hospitals, navigating the floors, nursing stations and discharge planning departments to become an indispensable resource to clinical staff.

Your primary mission is to educate hospital teams on Abby Care’s capabilities, build deep-rooted relationships from the ground up, and ultimately drive a high volume of qualified patient referrals. You will act as the crucial bridge between a patient's hospital discharge and their successful transition to home care. Because this is a true growth role, your success, goals, and compensation are directly tied to the sustained referral volume you generate from your assigned facilities.

Your Responsibilities

 - Embed & Map the Facility: Maintain a highly visible, daily presence within your assigned hospitals. Identify and map out key decision-makers, including discharge planners, social workers, bedside nurses, and attending physicians.

 - Educate & Advocate: Serve as the subject matter expert on Abby Care. Conduct formal presentations and informal floor-level education so staff immediately recognize when a patient is a perfect fit for our programs.

 - Drive Referral Volume: Actively prospect for eligible patients and build a predictable pipeline. You will be held accountable to strict volume metrics, and your compensation will scale with the referrals you bring in.

 - Streamline Transitions: Remove friction in the admissions process. Work closely with hospital staff and the internal Abby Care intake team to ensure a seamless, urgent handoff from the hospital bed to the home.

 - Track & Report: Rigorously utilize our CRM to log facility activities, track relationship progress, manage your pipeline, and analyze growth metrics within your assigned hospitals.

What you’ll have:

 - 4+ years of experience in a hospital or healthcare role, with a track record of building relationships across multidisciplinary teams including discharge planners, social workers and nursing staff.

 - You have experience being embedded in the floors, nursing stations and discharge planning rooms where decisions actually get made.

 - The experience of being genuinely essential. Clinical teams will come to rely on you as a trusted resource who solves placement problems and makes transitions smoother for patients and staff alike.

 - Mastery of clinical environments and patient transitions.

THE ROLE

We're looking for a passionate Registered Nurse to join our team as a RN Case Manager. This role will report into the Director of Nursing in Massachusetts. This is a Full-Time opportunity based in the Worcester County North/Fitchburg Area.

The RN Case Manager at Abby Care provides the comprehensive care and coordination of services for patients within their caseload. This role requires a nurse with excellent organizational skills, strong communication abilities, and a dedication to providing high-quality patient care. The RN Case Manager will work closely with patients, families, Primary Care Physicians (PCPs), therapy providers, and internal support teams to ensure optimal patient outcomes.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Patient Care and Coordination:
   
   - Manage a diverse caseload, ensuring quality patient care and efficient care coordination.
   
   - Provide coaching, oversight, and support to Home Health Aides (HHAs) to ensure high-quality patient care and adherence to clinical standards.
   
   - Develop and implement individualized care plans based on patient assessments and goals.
   
   - Coordinate with PCPs, therapy providers, and internal support teams to ensure comprehensive care.
   
   - Provide education and support to patients and families regarding their health care needs and treatment plans.

 - Clinical Documentation:
   
   - Maintain accurate and timely electronic charting, with a preference for real-time documentation.
   
   - Ensure all documentation meets regulatory and Abby Care standards, with a minimum of 24-hour turnaround time.

 - Communication and Collaboration:
   
   - Establish and maintain effective communication with PCPs, therapy providers, and internal support teams.
   
   - Escalate appropriate issues to Clinical Leads in a timely manner, such as order management and patient concerns.
   
   - Participate in on-call rotation (approximately one weekend per month), holiday rotation, and possible phone triage night coverage during weekdays.

 - Quality Assurance:
   
   - Achieve caregiver satisfaction by providing high-quality, compassionate care and support.
   
   - Monitor and work to improve patient readmission rates.
   
   - Ensure extended visits remain within acceptable ranges and administrative tasks (e.g., mileage reimbursements) are completed thoroughly.
     
     


THE REQUIREMENTS:

 - An Associate's Degree or A Baccalaureate School of Nursing Degree.

 - Current and valid license as a Registered Nurse in the state of Massachusetts.

 - Bilingual in spanish (preferred)

 - 2 years of experience as a Registered Nurse in a clinical setting; 1 year of home health experience preferred.

 - Case management/supervi

Join us in solving one of the most important challenges of our time.






THE ROLE

The Strategic Partnerships Liaison is the growth engine for Abby Care within the community case management ecosystem. Your mission is to build, own, and scale referral-generating relationships with case management professionals and organizations. Unlike traditional sales, this role requires a deeply consultative approach. You aren't just pitching a service; you are educating partners, solving their placement challenges, and becoming their absolute go-to resource for patient care.

Your goal is to spread awareness, build immense trust, and drive a sustained, high volume of referrals to Abby Care. Because this is a fundamental growth position, your performance targets and compensation structure are directly linked to the referral volume and successful admissions generated from your territory.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build & Own Relationships: Manage the entire lifecycle of partner relationships in your territory, from initial cold outreach and discovery to ongoing account management and referral troubleshooting.

 - Execute Market-Specific Strategies:
   
   - Navigate Payer organizations to connect directly with embedded Payer Case Managers and their leadership, bypassing broad corporate structures. Additionally, build strong partnerships with Support Coordinators and Support Coordination Organizations (SCOs) under the ODP Program.

 - Consultative Education: Teach case managers exactly who fits our programs, how to initiate a referral effortlessly, and why Abby Care delivers the best outcomes for the families they manage.

 - Drive Targeted Referral Volume: Meet and exceed ambitious referral quotas. You will be compensated based on the success and volume of the relationships you build.

 - Field-Based Activity: Maintain a highly active field schedule. Prioritize face-to-face meetings, community networking, and regular check-ins over office time.

 - Pipeline Management: Treat your territory like a business. Rigorously track all partner interactions, account statuses, and referral conversion metrics in our CRM.




WHAT YOU’LL HAVE:

 - 4+ years of experience navigating Payer organizations, managed care environments, or ODP/waiver-funded services. With a proven ability to build relationships inside complex, layered organizations rather than just at the surface level.

 - An understanding of Pennsylvania's Payer and ODP ecosystem. Experience working with or alongside Payer Case Managers, Support Coordinators and Support Coordination Organizations is a strong advantage.

 - The skill to streamline bureaucracy and reach decision makers. This market rewards people who know how to navigate organizations strategically and build trust at every level.

 - Territory owner

THE ROLE

This role will initially function as a hands-on Revenue Cycle Management (RCM) Lead responsible for establishing billing operations for a newly launched Georgia and New Jersey market. The successful candidate will build processes, conduct payer and regulatory research, create SOPs, and lay the foundation for a scalable RCM department. As the market grows, this role will evolve into a formal people leadership position overseeing billing specialists and offshore team members.





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 1. New Department Development
    
    - Lead the establishment of revenue cycle operations for a newly launched market, including researching payer requirements, workflows, billing processes, and reimbursement guidelines.
    
    - Develop, document, and implement SOPs, workflows, and operational best practices to support future team growth.
    
    - Research and interpret Georgia and New Jersey Medicaid billing requirements and identify operational requirements necessary to support compliance and reimbursement.
    
    - Partner with leadership to define team structure, operational processes, performance metrics, and future KPIs as the department scales.
    
    - Identify process gaps and recommend solutions to improve operational efficiency and future revenue cycle performance.

 2. Team Leadership:
    
    - Initially operate as an individual contributor while building the foundation of the department.
    
    - Transition into a people leadership role as the Georgia and New Jersey markets expand, including hiring, training, coaching, and managing billing team members.
    
    - Provide mentorship, onboarding support, and performance coaching to future team members.
    
    - Experience conducting performance reviews is highly preferred.

 3. Billing Operations:
    
    - Oversee the preparation, submission, and follow-up of insurance claims for home health care services.
    
    - Ensure accurate coding (CPT, HCPCS, ICD-10) and adherence to payer-specific guidelines.
    
    - Monitor and resolve claim rejections, denials, and underpayments promptly.
    
    - Manage accounts receivable to minimize outstanding balances and maximize collections.

 4. Compliance and Documentation:
    
    - Ensure billing practices comply with federal, state, and payer-specific regulations, including Medicare and Medicaid guidelines.
    
    - Maintain up-to-date knowledge of changes in billing rules and home health care regulations.
    
    - Review documentation for accuracy and completeness to support submitted claims.

 5. Process Improvement:
    
    - Analyze billing processes and implement strategies to improve efficiency and reduce errors.

The RN Case Manager at Abby Care provides the comprehensive care and coordination of services for patients within their caseload. This role requires a nurse with excellent organizational skills, strong communication abilities, and a dedication to providing high-quality patient care. The RN Case Manager will work closely with patients, families, Primary Care Physicians (PCPs), therapy providers, and internal support teams to ensure optimal patient outcomes.

 
 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Patient Care and Coordination:
   
   - Manage a diverse caseload, ensuring quality patient care and efficient care coordination.

   - Ensure all documentation meets regulatory and Abby Care standards, with a minimum of 24-hour turnaround time.

 - Communication and Collaboration:
   
   - Establish and maintain effective communication with PCPs, therapy providers, and internal support teams.
   
   - Escalate appropriate issues to Clinical Leads in a timely manner, such as order management and patient concerns.
   
   - Participate in on-call rotation (approximately one weekend per month), holiday rotation, and possible phone triage night coverage during weekdays.

 - Quality Assurance:
   
   - Achieve caregiver satisfaction by providing high-quality, compassionate care and support.
   
   - Monitor and work to improve patient readmission rates.
   
   - Ensure extended visits remain within acceptable ranges and administrative tasks (e.g., mileage reimbursements) are completed thoroughly.
    


THE REQUIREMENTS:

 - An Associate's Degree or A Baccalaureate School of Nursing Degree.

We believe families are the largest untapped caregiving workforce in America, and that technology can help them deliver better care while driving stronger outcomes and greater transparency across the healthcare system.

Abby Care combines clinical oversight with an AI-powered platform to train, enable, and support family caregivers in delivering high-quality care at home. Our platform helps health plans and government partners better understand, verify, and improve care in the home. We expand access to care, reduce reliance on higher-cost settings, and help ensure public dollars are spent effectively.

We are proud to partner with leading health plans, providers, and community organizations and are backed by top VCs. We envision a future where family-led care is a core part of the healthcare system. Abby Care is building that future.

Join us in solving one of the most important challenges of our time.




THE ROLE

We're looking for a high-potential Executive Assistant to support three of our key executives: Head of Engineering, Head of Product, and Head of People. This in-person role (4x a week) is based in San Francisco.

You take pride in a well-run calendar, a clean inbox, and a flawlessly executed offsite. You see the unglamorous work as the work. You will own the calendars, inbox, travel, and operating cadence of three executives, and you will also own a defined portfolio of operational projects that scale company-wide. The EA craft is the core of this role. The ops portfolio is additive.

You'll report to the Sr. Executive Assistant and work as a direct extension of three of our most important leaders. This is the right role for someone with a few years of EA experience who wants to grow their scope inside a fast-moving company, with a real path to broader admin and operational leadership over time.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

 - Own the calendars of three executives. Manage scheduling, travel, and meeting prep for the Head of Engineering, Head of Product, and Head of People. Resolve conflicts independently. Anticipate two to three weeks out, not two days out.

 - Run the inbox and information flow. Triage inbound, draft responses, and ensure nothing falls through the cracks across all three principals.

 - Drive the operating cadence. Own prep, agendas, and follow-through for leadership team meetings, engineering and product offsites, and key external engagements.

 - Own a defined operational portfolio. Lead all-hands logistics, engineering and product offsite execution, recruiting coordination for technical and people hires, and exec-level new hire onboarding.

 - Handle confidential work with discretion. Particularly on the People side, you will see comp, performance, and HR-sensitive information. We expect total discretion.

 - Partner with the Sr. EA. Operate as part of a tight admin team. Cover for the Sr. EA when needed. Help raise the bar on how Abby Care runs its executive operations end to end.




THE REQUIREMENTS:

 - At least 2 years of experience supporting executives at high-growth startups, top-tier professional services firms, or similarly high-velocity environments. We are not tied to a specific number of years, but rather the quality of your experience and outcomes.

 - Strong written communication, comfortable drafting on behalf of executives in internal and external contexts

THE OPPORTUNITY:

As we expand our capacity to support more family caregivers at scale, we’re growing the teams that support this work. We're looking for a high-agency operator to join us as our Senior Privacy & AI Counsel. Reporting to the General Counsel, you'll be the day-to-day owner of Abby Care’s privacy and AI governance programs in a high-tempo, rapidly evolving environment where the regulatory landscape shifts fast and ambiguity is the norm, not the exception.

Sitting at the intersection of strategy and execution, this is a senior, build-and-lead role. While our privacy program has a working foundation, our next phase—broader AI deployment, new state environments, and a denser vendor ecosystem—requires a senior lawyer who can set program strategy, run day-to-day operations, and serve as a true subject matter expert. You won't just be reviewing individual cases; you will own the AI governance program, scale our HIPAA Privacy program, and partner closely with the GC, Product, Engineering, Operations, and Clinical leadership to unlock safe, compliant growth. Alongside the GC, you will also brief the Executive team and Board on privacy and AI risk.

A true player-coach, you are equally comfortable setting high-level program strategy as you are rolling up your sleeves to handle daily reviews. Highly collaborative, you find energy working across diverse, cross-functional teams toward a common goal. If you are excited by the opportunity to build the playbook for AI and privacy at scale, all while transforming care at home for vulnerable populations, we’d love to hear from you!

This is a Full-Time Hybrid opportunity based in San Francisco.

What you’ll work on:

 - Own Abby Care's privacy program — HIPAA compliance, state privacy law compliance, BAA program, data mapping and ROPA, privacy incident response, breach assessment and notification, and individual rights workflows — including its design, operation, measurement, and continuous improvement.

 - Own Abby Care's AI governance program — the responsible AI policy suite, AI inventory, AI use case intake and review process, model risk classification, ongoing monitoring, and AI incident response — and evolve it as the regulatory and deployment landscape shifts.
